summary: Teams Management fixes verified - All 12 backend tests pass (1 minor assertion issue), UI verified with all key features working. Teams page loads with 10 teams, all 8 departments available, leader/member modals show ALL users without role filter, search functionality working in both modals, N+1 query fixes confirmed for GET /api/teams (leader_name, member_count) and GET /api/leads (assigned_to_name, course_name).

minor.issue: User's team_id field not returned in GET /api/users for pre-existing users without team_id - this is data initialization issue, not API bug. The POST /api/teams/{id}/members API works correctly.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/teams returns list of teams
Backend: GET /api/teams has leader_name field (N+1 fix verified)
Backend: GET /api/teams has member_count field (N+1 fix verified)
Backend: POST /api/teams creates team with Mentors/Academics department
Backend: POST /api/teams accepts all 8 departments
Backend: GET /api/teams/{id} returns team details with members
Backend: GET /api/users returns ALL active users (no role filter)
Backend: PUT /api/teams/{id} assigns leader and updates user role to team_leader
Backend: POST /api/teams/{id}/members adds user to team
Backend: GET /api/leads returns assigned_to_name (N+1 fix verified)
Backend: GET /api/leads returns course_name (N+1 fix verified)
Backend: Users have full_name and email for search functionality
Frontend: Teams Management page loads correctly (10 teams)
Frontend: Create Team modal opens with department dropdown
Frontend: All 8 departments visible in dropdown including 'Mentors/Academics'
Frontend: Teams list shows leader_name and member_count
Frontend: Selecting team shows Team Details panel with leader and members
Frontend: Assign Leader modal opens with search input (data-testid='search-leader-input')
Frontend: Assign Leader shows ALL users (not filtered by role)
Frontend: Search in Leader modal filters by name/email
Frontend: Add Member modal opens with search input (data-testid='search-member-input')
Frontend: Add Member shows all active users from Employee Master
Frontend: Search in Member modal filters by name/email
Frontend: Member list correctly excludes users already in team

teams_page_loading.details: Teams page loads with 10 teams, left panel shows team list with leader name and member count, right panel shows selected team details

departments_list.details: Create Team modal dropdown shows all 8 departments including Mentors/Academics

assign_leader_modal.details: Shows ALL active users without role filter, search input filters by name/email, clicking user assigns them as team leader and updates their role to 'team_leader'

add_member_modal.details: Shows all active users not already in team, search input filters by name/email, clicking user adds them to team and updates their team_id

n_plus_1_query_fixes.teams_endpoint: GET /api/teams returns leader_name (batch fetched from users) and member_count (aggregated from users)
n_plus_1_query_fixes.leads_endpoint: GET /api/leads returns assigned_to_name (batch fetched) and course_name (batch fetched)

critical_code_review_comments: N+1 fix in GET /api/teams uses batch queries for leader names and member counts - efficient implementation
potentialLeaders in TeamsPage.jsx correctly filters only by is_active (no role restriction)
availableUsers in TeamsPage.jsx correctly excludes users already in selected team's members
Both search inputs have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Department dropdown uses proper case strings like 'Mentors/Academics' (not snake_case)
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created teams for all 8 departments with names prefixed 'TEST_Dept_Team_'. These are visible in the Teams page.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Teams Management feature fully tested. All 8 departments work, leader/member assignment modals show ALL users without role filters, search functionality works in both modals. N+1 query fixes verified for teams (leader_name, member_count) and leads (assigned_to_name, course_name). Test teams exist in database with 'TEST_' prefix. The Customer Service test team has 2 members and a leader (John Test Employee).
testing_method: Backend pytest (13 tests) + Playwright browser automation for UI verification with screenshots
